behaviors.description: Static prompt-template lint rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is template/lint rule logic at one object boundary. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none required. Rejection gate: higher levels were rejected because behavior text says "static prompt-template lint check" (no harness/runtime/model dependency).
behaviors.test_description: Call the lint function on template strings: one containing copied phase instructions from SKILL.md and one clean control. Assert leakage case is rejected with precise rule ID/location.

behaviors.description: Fully assembled prompt from the real harness (post interpolation/fixtures) contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: bug appears at module handoff (template + interpolator + fixture merger). REAL-INFRA: local wiring only; no OS/network/subprocess requirement. LLM-DEP: none. Rejection gate: System/Agentic rejected because behavior text is about assembled prompt content before model execution.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real prompt-construction pipeline with representative fixtures and interpolation variables, then assert assembled prompt does not include forbidden phase-instruction snippets or markers sourced from SKILL.md text.

behaviors.description: Test invocation loads skill context from plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context, not hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is cross-module (skill loader -> prompt/context injector -> invocation path). REAL-INFRA: local filesystem is sufficient; no external system needed. LLM-DEP: behavior can be validated via source-of-truth and injection checks without relying on model judgment. Rejection gate: Agentic rejected because text emphasizes context source correctness, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Execute invocation with real skill file loading enabled; assert context payload originates from plugin/skill files (e.g., file hash/path provenance) and that no hard-coded phase rules are present in the test prompt template.

behaviors.description: Harness runs the same user-style prompt against intentionally degraded or missing SKILL.md variants without changing prompt text.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is harness orchestration across variant selection + loader + invocation config. REAL-INFRA: local file variants only. LLM-DEP: no requirement to judge model quality here, only invariant prompt text and variant switching. Rejection gate: Agentic rejected because behavior text targets harness capability (same prompt, changed skill variant), not response correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Parameterize test over baseline/degraded/missing SKILL.md fixtures; run harness each time with identical user prompt string and assert prompt text bytes are unchanged while loaded skill context source differs by variant.

behaviors.description: When relevant SKILL.md content is removed/degraded, behavioral test fails under the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: pass/fail depends on whether real model behavior changes when skill context degrades; stubs would hide this. MULTI-STEP: single model decision is sufficient, so Workflow not required. Rejection gate: Integration rejected because text explicitly requires behavioral failure due to degraded SKILL.md under same prompt.
behaviors.test_description: Run live model with baseline skill and degraded/missing variant using identical user prompt; apply the same behavioral assertion suite and require degraded variant to fail (or materially regress) while baseline passes.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before JSON parsing/extraction logic.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: ordering bug occurs at boundary between model client, recorder, and parser modules. REAL-INFRA: local artifact write path is enough; no external system needed. LLM-DEP: capture ordering can be tested with deterministic fake response. Rejection gate: Agentic rejected because behavior is pipeline instrumentation order, not model-content correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke response pipeline with a controlled model output; assert raw artifact is persisted first (timestamp/order marker) and matches pre-parse bytes, then parser runs and can fail independently without losing artifact.

behaviors.description: Assertions enforce structural constraints (schema paths, role-specific field placement), not only keyword presence.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: core failure is assertion logic correctness on given payloads. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none required for this behavior itself. Rejection gate: Integration rejected because behavior text focuses on assertion semantics, which can be validated on local fixtures.
behaviors.test_description: Feed assertion module crafted payloads: keyword-present but structurally invalid, and structurally valid controls. Assert invalid cases fail on schema path/role placement errors even when keywords match.

behaviors.description: Under live model execution, structural assertions catch regressions that keyword-only checks miss.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: requires real model variability/content to produce cases where keywords pass but structure regresses. MOCK-HIDE: mocked outputs can be curated to pass, hiding true regressions. MULTI-STEP: single live generation + assertion is enough; no multi-agent chain required. Rejection gate: Integration rejected by explicit text "Under live model execution".
behaviors.test_description: Run live model prompts representative of the skill behavior; evaluate outputs with both keyword-only and structural assertion suites; assert at least one live regression is caught only by structural checks.
